class.name: PlayFabParty
class.brief_description: PlayFab Party network and chat service.
class.description: Accessed through [code]PlayFab.party[/code]. Owns PlayFab Party initialization, network host/join flows, descriptor serialization, and chat-control integration. The title-facing transport object is [PlayFabPartyPeer], assigned to [code]multiplayer.multiplayer_peer[/code] for RPC traffic and exposing peer-id helpers for text chat, mute, and permissions. PlayFab Multiplayer lobbies and matchmaking are handled separately by [PlayFabMultiplayer].

method.name: is_initialized
method.description: Returns whether the PlayFab Party service has been initialized.

method.name: initialize_async
method.description: Initializes [code]PartyManager[/code] under the root PlayFab runtime. Must be called after [PlayFab] is initialized. [param local_udp_port] is an optional per-call override for [code]PartyOption::LocalUdpSocketBindAddress[/code]: [code]-1[/code] (default) defers to the [code]playfab/party/local_udp_socket_bind_port[/code] project setting (which itself defaults to [code]-1[/code] meaning "use the SDK's default bind"); [code]0[/code] asks the OS to pick an ephemeral port and excludes the Game Core preferred multiplayer port (the right choice for multi-process same-host scenarios such as CI test orchestrators or splitscreen dev iteration); a specific value [code]1[/code]-[code]65535[/code] pins to that port.

method.name: shutdown_async
method.description: Closes active Party networks, endpoints, and chat controls and shuts down [code]PartyManager[/code].

method.name: create_and_join_network_async
method.description: Creates a Party network for [param user] and resolves with a ready [PlayFabPartyNetwork] whose [PlayFabPartyPeer] is host (peer id [code]1[/code]).

method.name: join_network_async
method.description: Joins an existing Party network using a base64 descriptor obtained from a host's [PlayFabPartyNetwork].

method.name: leave_network_async
method.description: Leaves [param network], closing endpoints, chat controls, and the local Party user reference if no other network is active.

method.name: get_chat
method.description: Returns the diagnostic [PlayFabPartyChat] surface for advanced chat-control access.

method.name: get_networks
method.description: Returns active [PlayFabPartyNetwork] instances tracked by the service.

constants.constant: No direct peer connectivity; all traffic relays through PlayFab Party. This is the default and is always valid by itself.

Allow direct peer connectivity to peers on the same platform type. Must be combined with at least one entity-login-provider flag (e.g. [constant DIRECT_PEER_CONNECTIVITY_ANY_ENTITY_LOGIN_PROVIDER]); not valid by itself.
Allow direct peer connectivity to peers on a different platform type. Must be combined with at least one entity-login-provider flag; not valid by itself.

Allow direct peer connectivity to peers on any platform type ([code]SAME_PLATFORM_TYPE | DIFFERENT_PLATFORM_TYPE[/code]). Must be combined with at least one entity-login-provider flag; not valid by itself. For the common "any platform, any login provider" preset, use [constant DIRECT_PEER_CONNECTIVITY_ANY] instead.

Allow direct peer connectivity to peers whose authenticated PlayFab entity used the same login provider (Xbox Live, Facebook, iOS, Google, etc.) as the local user. Must be combined with at least one platform-type flag; not valid by itself.
Allow direct peer connectivity to peers whose authenticated PlayFab entity used a different login provider. Must be combined with at least one platform-type flag; not valid by itself.

Allow direct peer connectivity regardless of the entity login provider ([code]SAME_ENTITY_LOGIN_PROVIDER | DIFFERENT_ENTITY_LOGIN_PROVIDER[/code]). Must be combined with at least one platform-type flag; not valid by itself.

Convenience preset that attempts direct peer connectivity across any platform type and any entity login provider ([code]ANY_PLATFORM_TYPE | ANY_ENTITY_LOGIN_PROVIDER[/code]). Use this when you want the broadest direct-connection opportunity without composing flags manually.

Only attempt direct peer connectivity when one of the endpoints belongs to a [code]game_server[/code] PlayFab entity. Cannot be combined with any platform-type or entity-login-provider flag in the network configuration; use it by itself.
Party network is being created.
Party network is connecting.
Local Party user is authenticating.
Party network is fully connected and ready for traffic.
Party network is disconnecting.
Party network has disconnected.
Party network entered a terminal failure state.
No chat permissions granted.
Local user may send voice audio to the target peer.
Local user may receive voice audio from the target peer.

Local user may receive text chat from the target peer. (Sending text is per-call: target recipients are listed in the [code]targets[/code] argument of [code]send_text_async()[/code]; there is no separate send-text permission flag.)
